title: Macrophage-Based Assay for Evaluating Compound Activity Against Leishmania Amastigotes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"du6iq","text":"This protocol describes a macrophage-based assay for evaluating anti-leishmanial compound activity against intracellular amastigotes. BALB/c mouse-derived macrophages are seeded in 8-well chamber slides and infected with Leishmaniaparasites using either parasites isolated from infected mice or PNA-selected metacyclic promastigotes from laboratory cultures (for L. major only). Following infection, macrophages are treated with test compounds, then fixed and stained with Giemsa for microscopic evaluation. This assay assesses compound efficacy against the clinically relevant amastigote stage within the host cell environment. The complete process takes 4-5 days, with an additional 8 days required for macrophage differentiation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":221,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":363,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5kh3e","text":"Before starting, make sure macrophages were obtained correctly from Balb/C mice for optimal infection. Keep in mind the whole process of monocyte isolation and differentiation into macrophages takes approximately 8 days.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ojpk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4otlc","text":"Also, you need to have infective parasites already in culture. If they are isolated directly from infective mice then the infection will be optimal. You can also opt to select infective L. major parasites from a lab culture wt strain if you add Peanut agglutinin (PNA) to the culture (see other protocols for this). Normally, 10% of a lab culture wt strain will be infective after 2 days of culture without renovation of medium (to let them enter the metacyclic phase).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ig14","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vfcc","text":"You will need a considerable amount of PBS so check you have it available. Also, remember to pre-warm it before the start of the experiment (so that the macrophages do not detach from the labtecks). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bsvqn","text":"DO NOT WASH MACROPHAGES WITH COLD PBS. IT SHOULD ALWAYS BE WARM (37 C). If not, they can detach. Also, make sure it does not have","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4018m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"73q69","text":"Follow closely the timing, since more incubation time with methanol or Giemsa could spoil the staining. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Place the Balb/C derived Macrophages in ice for 6 minutes
Scrape them gently and pass the contents to a 50 mL centrifugation tube
Centrifuge the cells at 500 x g, 4 °C, 7 minutes
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet with 1 mL of DMEM + 10% FBS + 1 % Streptomycin + Penicillin (from now on, whnever DMEM appears, it refers to DMEM + FBS + Streptomycin and penicillin)
Count the cells in a Neubauer chamber
Calculate how much DMEM you should add to the pellet to obtain a 5 x 10 5 cells/mL solution. Add it.
Seed 100 µL of the 5 x10 5 cells/mL solution into each well.
The following steps depend on the Leishmania species you are working with and if they are lab strains or extracted from infected mice
Incubate for 24:00:00 at 37 °C

If you are working with any Leishmania species isolated from infected mice then wait until the parasites acquire an elongated shape. This means they are ready for infection. Normally, it takes around 10 days of culturing in 10 mL of Schneider (add medium progressively, every day, until you reach 10 mL) since isolation to acquire this infective form.
The day of Labteck infection centrifuge the cells at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Stay with the pellet and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of DMEM and count in a Neubauer.

Calculate how much DMEM you need in total ( 100 µL per well). Then how many µL you should take from the pellet to obtain a concentration of 1 x10 7 parasites/mL. This means we are infecting in a 1:20 ratio (20 parasites per macrophage)
Add 100 µL of the 1 x10 7 parasites/mL solution to each well (this means we are adding 1 x 10 6 parasites per well).
Incubate for at 37 °C for 24:00:00
If you are working with L. major (this will not work with other species), then you can select the infective form with Peanut agglutinin (PNA).
The day of Labteck infection centrifuge the cells in a 50 mL tube at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes . Stay with the pellet and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of DMEM and count in a Neubauer.

Calculate how much DMEM you need in total ( 100 µL per well). Then how many µL you should take from the pellet to obtain a concentration of 1 x10 7 parasites/mL. This means we are infecting in a 1:20 ratio (20 parasites per macrophage)

Add 100 µL of the 1 x10 7 parasites/mL solution to each well (this means we are adding 1 x 10 6 parasites per well). Note that since we already had 1 100 µL in the wells from the macrophage seeding, now we have a final volume of 200 µL in each well.
Incubate at 37 °C for 24:00:00

Add 100 µg of PNA per 500 x 10 6 parasites and let it incubate at Room temperature for 00:25:00 . Since PNA is sensible to light, you should cover the falcon with aluminum foil. Our laboratory has PNA stocks at 5000 ug/mL, so we have a rule of thumb: the amount of PNA needed (in µl) would follow: \text{PNA} (\mu g) = \frac{\# \text{ parasites}}{2.5 \times 10^{10}}
After incubation, add ( SLOWLY ) DMEM to the pellet with a pipette. Do it touching the wall of the Falcon, to avoid disturbing the pellet.
Now we have 11 mL of supernatant. Take it gently and pass it to another 50 mL Falcon for centrifugation at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .
Before treatment, pre warm PBS and DMEM at 37 °C

Proceed to remove the 200 µL of DMEM from each well with a pasteur pipette. Do it gently and from one of the corners of each well. Be consistent: if you chose the lower left corner for the first well, do the same for the rest.

Now add warm PBS to each well for washing. Remove it and repeat the process 3 times (a total of 3 PBS washes). The last wash you should do with a p1000 micropipette to remove all the content and leave no trace. Before proceeding, go to the microscope and check that there are no parasites left in one of the wells (the rest should be simmilar). If there is a considerable amount, then repeat the washing until there are almost no parasites left floating.

Also before proceeding, calculate how much compound you should dilute in warm DMEM to obtain the desired concentration at a final volume of 200 µL per well. Take in mind you need negative control wells (with no treatment), positive controls and replicates.
Add 200 µL of compound dilution to each well. Don't forget negative controls ( 200 µL of DMEM per well) and positive controls.
Incubate for 48:00:00 at 37 °C (or whatever treatment regime you choose, 12h, 24h...).
When the treatment incubation time ends, wash the wells with warm PBS (see steps 22 and 23)
Add 300 µL MetOH to each well and leave it 5 minutes
Dilute Giemsa in water 1:20 (20 mL of water per mL of Giemsa).
Add 200 µL of the 1:20 Giemsa solution to each well and incubate for 00:20:00

Wash with distilled water. You can use three beakers full with water and simply submerge each Lab-tek in a stepwise manner. Submerge each lab-tek 5 in the first beaker, then pass to the next beaker (5 times) and then to the third beaker (5 more times).
Before treatment, pre warm PBS and DMEM at 37 °C

Also before proceeding, calculate how much compound you should dilute in warm DMEM to obtain the desired concentration at a final volume of 200 µL per well. Take in mind you need negative control wells (with no treatment), positive controls and replicates.

Proceed to remove the 200 µL of DMEM from each well with a pasteur pipette. Do it gently and from one of the corners of each well. Be consistent: if you chose the lower left corner for the first well, do the same for the rest.

Now add warm PBS to each well for washing. Remove it and repeat the process 3 times (a total of 3 PBS washes). The last wash you should do with a p1000 micropipette to remove all the content and leave no trace. Before proceeding, go to the microscope and check that there are no parasites left in one of the wells (the rest should be simmilar). If there is a considerable amount, then repeat the washing until there are almost no parasites left floating.
Add 200 µL of compound dilution to each well. Don't forget negative controls ( 200 µL of DMEM per well) and positive controls.
Incubate for 48:00:00 at 37 °C (or whatever treatment regime you choose, 12h, 24h...).
When the treatment incubation time ends, wash the wells with warm PBS (see steps 22 and 23)
Add 300 µL MetOH to each well and leave it 5 minutes
Dilute Giemsa in water 1:20 (20 mL of water per mL of Giemsa).
Add 200 µL of the 1:20 Giemsa solution to each well and incubate for 00:20:00

Wash with distilled water. You can use three beakers full with water and simply submerge each Lab-tek in a stepwise manner. Submerge each lab-tek 5 in the first beaker, then pass to the next beaker (5 times) and then to the third beaker (5 more times).
Wait until the slides are dry. Follow the instruction from Lab-tek to remove the chamber walls. Then, mount the cover slides with mounting glue.
Wait until the slides are dry. Follow the instruction from Lab-tek to remove the chamber walls. Then, mount the cover slides with mounting glue.
